payload.title: Every Java program has a main method
payload.body: Java needs a class and a main method to run. System.out.println() is how you print text. It looks long, but you'll memorize it fast!

payload.prompt: How do you print text in Java?
payload.explanation: System.out.println() prints a line of text in Java.
println prints text and moves to the next line.

lessons.title: Types & Variables
lessons.summary: Declare variables with their type in Java.

payload.title: Java is a typed language
payload.body: In Java, you must say what type a variable is before naming it. int for whole numbers, double for decimals, String for text, boolean for true/false.

payload.prompt: Which type holds whole numbers in Java?
payload.explanation: int stores whole numbers like 0, 42, or -7.

payload.prompt: Declare a whole-number variable called lives with value 3.
payload.explanation: int is the type for whole numbers in Java.

payload.title: Strings hold text
payload.body: A String in Java is text surrounded by double quotes. You can join two strings with + — called concatenation.

payload.prompt: How do you join two Strings in Java?
payload.explanation: The + operator concatenates (joins) Strings in Java.

payload.prompt: Join the two strings with the correct operator.

payload.title: Branching with if / else
payload.body: Java uses curly braces { } to group the code that runs when a condition is true. else runs when it's false.

payload.prompt: In Java, what wraps the condition in an if statement?
payload.explanation: The condition goes in ( ) and the code block goes in { }.

payload.prompt: Choose the correct comparison operator for 'greater than or equal to'.

lessons.title: For Loops
lessons.summary: Repeat code with a counting loop.

payload.title: The classic Java for loop
payload.body: Java's for loop has three parts: start, condition, step. It's used everywhere — from printing tables to processing lists.

payload.prompt: What does i++ mean in a Java for loop?
payload.explanation: i++ is shorthand for i = i + 1 — it increments i by 1 each iteration.

payload.prompt: Pick the correct step expression to count up by 1.
payload.explanation: i++ increments i by 1 after each loop iteration.

lessons.title: Methods
lessons.summary: Write a reusable method in Java.

payload.title: Methods are Java's functions
payload.body: A method is a named block of code you can call anytime. void means it doesn't return a value. static lets you call it from main without creating an object.

payload.prompt: What does void mean in a method definition?
payload.explanation: void means the method performs an action but doesn't send a value back.

payload.prompt: Choose the return type for a method that prints but returns nothing.
payload.explanation: void signals the method gives nothing back to the caller.
